evals.prompt: We ran the coverage audit last week and exported the CSV to ~/Downloads/coverage-audit-2026-06-20.csv. We're still waiting on the internal Codecov instance to be stood up — could be a few weeks. Can you open prepare PRs for all the repos marked Onboard=TRUE so we're ready to flip the switch when the instance is live?
evals.expected_output: Reads the CSV, filters to Onboard=TRUE rows where Has Codecov is not TRUE, announces the count, dispatches one subagent per repo in parallel running prepare mode, and prints a summary table of opened MRs/PRs. Each PR contains disabled CI job (when: never for GitLab or if: false for GitHub), coverage flags added to existing test command, and codecov.yml. No PRs are opened in dry-run. Summary includes any repos needing manual attention.

evals.prompt: The internal Codecov instance just went live at https://codecov.internal.example.com. CONFIG.md has been updated with the URL. We've got about 30 repos that had the prepare PRs merged last month. Can you go through the audit CSV at ./audit-q2.csv and open enable PRs for all of them?
evals.expected_output: Reads CONFIG.md to confirm the instance URL is set (not PLACEHOLDER). Reads the CSV, identifies rows with Onboard=TRUE and Has Codecov not TRUE (or checks progress file). Dispatches subagents in enable mode: each verifies the disabled job exists in the default branch, creates branch enable-codecov-coverage, removes when: never / if: false, fills in the real instance URL, commits, and opens an MR/PR. Prints a summary with opened MR/PR URLs and any skips.

evals.prompt: I want to set up Codecov for just one repo — https://gitlab.cee.redhat.com/myteam/myservice — but the instance isn't ready yet. The repo is a Go service using .gitlab-ci.yml. Can you open a prepare PR?
evals.expected_output: Single-repo mode. Checks for existing add-codecov-config branch. Clones the repo, creates branch add-codecov-config, finds the go test command in .gitlab-ci.yml, appends -coverprofile=coverage.out -covermode=atomic, reads the GitLab upload job template from codecov-onboarding Option C, applies the prepare modifier (when: never + CODECOV_URL: PLACEHOLDER), appends the job to .gitlab-ci.yml, adds codecov.yml from the add-codecov-yml template, commits, and opens an MR with the correct title and the PR description template body including the CODECOV_TOKEN manual step callout for GitLab.
